summary: Gruber argues that Twitter clients have become an ideal playground for UI design experimentation. The service's simplicity, combined with its massive popularity, has spawned dozens of clients with radically different design approaches — from information-dense TweetDeck to minimalist Tweetie. He notes that Twitter is uniquely well-suited to the iPhone, feeling unconstrained compared to email or web browsing on mobile. The low friction of switching between clients (just a username and password) creates healthy competition that drives design innovation.
key_points: Twitter's simple scope makes it a perfect UI design laboratory, yet the variety of approaches proves that even simple apps are hard to design well
Different clients serve fundamentally different needs — TweetDeck maximizes information density while Tweetie minimizes it — making 'best client' a meaningless question
Twitter is the one service where iPhone clients feel like full clients, not compromised mobile versions
Zero switching costs between Twitter clients create an unusually competitive and innovative design environment
The explosion of native Mac and iPhone clients represents a creative renaissance in app design
money_quotes: To paraphrase the great Yogi Berra, "Nobody uses Twitter anymore; it's too popular."
It is not easy to write a good client for something as small in scope as Twitter hints at just how hard it is to write a good app for anything, let alone something truly complex.
Asking for the "best Twitter client" is like asking for the "best shirt".
Twitter is the one service where reading on my iPhone doesn't feel constrained compared to reading on my Mac.
My favorite few iPhone Twitter clients are just plain good Twitter clients, with no need for a "for the iPhone" qualification.
One of the fantastic things about Twitter clients is how easy it is for users to jump from one to another. Just type in a username and password and off you go.
One could be forgiven for assuming the entire point of Air is to serve as a runtime for cross-platform Twitter clients.
key_insight: Twitter's simplicity, popularity, and zero switching costs have made it a uniquely fertile playground for UI design experimentation, revealing that even the simplest apps demand thoughtful, varied design approaches.
